stepAnswer: Our answer does not change. If we use a different confidence level, both the intervals at $1,100 and $700 will change in width, but the $700 interval will always be wider.
stepTitle: C2) Does the answer to the previous part change if we used a different confidence level? Why or why not?
stepBody: Assume the average price of a laptop in Milena’s dataset is $1,000. Milena generates 90% confidence intervals for the predicted battery life of laptops priced at $1,100 and $700.
choices: Our answer does not change. If we use a different confidence level, both the intervals at $1,100 and $700 will change in width, but the $700 interval will always be wider.
Our answer does change because the confidence level we use changes the reasoning in C1.
Our answer does not change because neither interval will change in width.
